name: Osaka Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: youth, progress, the sea, and the sky over Osaka

description: A blue field charged at the centre with a white prefectural emblem in the form of three stylised gourds arranged to suggest the kanji 大 (the first character of 'Osaka').
history: Osaka Prefecture's symbol (chōshō) was selected through a public competition and adopted on 25 June 1968 to commemorate the centenary of the prefecture's establishment. The flag, which uses the symbol on a blue field, has been in use as the prefectural flag since the same year. The emblem deliberately evokes Toyotomi Hideyoshi's sennari-byōtan war standard, used during the Sengoku period at Osaka Castle.

features.symbolism: the colour of progress and youth, chosen to represent Osaka's modern, forward-looking character

A stylised arrangement of three gourds derived from Toyotomi Hideyoshi's standard at Osaka Castle (the sennari-byōtan, 'thousand gourds'); the emblem was adopted in 1968 and arranges the three gourds to form the kanji 大 (ō, 'great') of 大阪 (Ōsaka).
